about.subtitle: Singer-songwriter · Luzern
about.bio1: Erik Sjøholm is a singer-songwriter and storyteller from Ostrobothnia — the Swedish-speaking coast of Finland — now based in Luzern, Switzerland. He writes songs that don't rush — music built from real moments, lived quietly and felt deeply.
about.bio2: Shaped by John Lennon, Jeff Buckley, Joni Mitchell, and Damien Rice, his sound carries the weight of a story you've heard before but can't quite place. Songs about love, loss, distance, and homecoming — and the thin line between all of them. He writes in English and Swedish, sometimes both in the same breath.
about.bio3: He has performed across Europe — from the Arbogast Festival in Sweden to Mezrab and the Amsterdam Storytelling Festival in the Netherlands, to storytelling gatherings in Croatia and Finland. His concert format GLENN weaves original songs with spoken narrative: every song earns its place in the room before it's played.
about.pullQuote: I think you deserve to be respected and loved for who you are.

concertSeries.desc1: An intimate concert series tied to the turning of the year — four evenings, four seasons, original songs written for the moment. Small rooms, close listening, no setlist repeated.

contact.songsForYouDesc: A song written for someone you love, for a moment that deserves its own music. Weddings, milestones, people who matter. Each one is written from scratch, for you alone.

storyteller.heroBio: A storytelling concert born on the day Erik Sjøholm came into the world — and his uncle Glenn left it.
storyteller.showDesc1: Imagine a world blanketed in snow, where light and darkness dance on the edge of silence. That is where Erik Sjøholm was born — and on that very day, his uncle Glenn took his own life, leaving behind a legacy of grief, questions, and unspoken stories.
storyteller.showDesc2: In this storytelling concert, Erik digs beneath the snow and ice, uncovering fragments of his family's unspoken mythology. Through raw personal stories and original songs — in the tradition of Damien Rice, Glen Hansard, and Jeff Buckley — he takes you on a journey into the heart of loss, self-discovery, and the search for meaning.

themes.title: Resilience
themes.sub: Rising from the ashes of pain

storyteller.trailerCaption: Performed at GLÖD Storytelling Festival, Vasa, Finland — March 2022

teamMembers.name: Erik Sjøholm
teamMembers.bio: Singer-songwriter and storyteller, Erik brings a raw personal narrative to the stage, blending music and storytelling in a way that moves audiences deeply.

teamMembers.name: Raphael Rodan
teamMembers.bio: An award-winning storyteller and theatre director based in the Netherlands. His work focuses on personal mythology and the universal stories that connect us.

teamMembers.name: Mey Rahimi
teamMembers.bio: A multidisciplinary artist specialising in visual storytelling and stage design. Her visual aesthetics elevate the emotional depth of the show.

storyteller.fundingNote: This storytelling concert has been partly funded by Svenska Kulturfonden.
